signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East tensions drive safe-haven demand, but 160.00 remains the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. The Fed-BoJ rate differential favors USD at 375bp — 159.46 holding below 160.00. Risk-off flow historically unwinds JPY shorts — 157.00 is the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +41,675 (bullish spec positioning) and retail 90% short — institutional vs retail divergence at extreme. HV30 at 6.8%, ATM IV at 8.1% — options pricing a 1.3 vol-pt premium over realized. 25d RR at −0.21: mild put skew despite the bullish price trend — market hedging rather than chasing. A LF delta turning positive or AM reducing would invalidate the caution read.

signals.title: EUR/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: ECB 2% vs Fed 3.75% — 175bp EUR carry disadvantage. EUR/USD at 1.1703, testing the 1.1700 level — holds above, but momentum is weak. A break below 1.1680 would signal further downside.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: GBP/USD COT LF net long +28,426 (bullish spec positioning). HV30 at 7.5%, ATM IV at 9.5% — options neutral. 25d RR flat — no directional bias. GBP/USD at 1.3488 mid-range; 1.3500 is the next resistance on a continuation.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT + OIS
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short −17,090 (specs short) vs RBNZ OIS hike 100% priced. Specs are fading a hawkish CB. If RBNZ delivers or signals hike, short squeeze risk is elevated.
